key_insight: The paper learns decision-dependent uncertainty from context and past residuals using nonparametric models, then embeds those predictions into a robust stochastic optimization framework with provable statistical guarantees.
engineering_relevance: This enables better real-world planning and control in systems where outcomes depend on both observed context and the decisions being made, such as supply chains, facility location, and pricing.
method: Use kNN, CART, or ReLU neural nets to model uncertainty as a function of decisions and context.
Apply ER-DD-SAA by adding empirical residuals to point predictions to account for estimation error.
For kNN two-stage problems, use a Benders decomposition plus constraint generation (BD-CG) algorithm with finite convergence guarantees.
evidence_signal: Experiments on a newsvendor-with-pricing problem and a two-stage facility location problem show improved out-of-sample performance over a parametric benchmark and better computational tractability.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of nonparametric learning and residual modeling, so scalability and tuning can be challenging in high-dimensional or data-sparse settings.
paper_title: Contextual Stochastic Optimization with Decision-Dependent Uncertainty via Nonparametric Learning
